forward_patch:
--- original
+++ modified
@@ -0,0 +1,8 @@
+---
+assignees:
+- claude-code
+position_column: todo
+position_ordinal: c380
+title: Navigation menu does not list the motion commands
+---
+The native Navigation menu does not show the motion/nav commands (nav.up/down/left/right/first/last/jump/drillIn/drillOut etc.) even though the keybindings work. The menu builder is not surfacing these commands. Investigate apps/kanban-app/src/menu.rs menu population vs the NAV_COMMAND_SPEC / command registry — the nav commands likely lack `menu` metadata or are filtered out of the menu build. They should appear under a Navigation menu with their accelerators.
\ No newline at end of file

The menu builder is not surfacing these commands. They should appear under a Navigation menu with their accelerators.
\ No newline at end of file

forward_patch:
--- original
+++ modified
@@ -5,4 +5,25 @@
 position_ordinal: c380
 title: Navigation menu does not list the motion commands
 ---
-The native Navigation menu does not show the motion/nav commands (nav.up/down/left/right/first/last/jump/drillIn/drillOut etc.) even though the keybindings work. The menu builder is not surfacing these commands. Investigate apps/kanban-app/src/menu.rs menu population vs the NAV_COMMAND_SPEC / command registry — the nav commands likely lack `menu` metadata or are filtered out of the menu build. They should appear under a Navigation menu with their accelerators.
\ No newline at end of file
+The native Navigation menu does not show the motion/nav commands (nav.up/down/left/right/first/last/jump/drillIn/drillOut etc.) even though the keybindings work.
+
+## Investigation finding (do NOT re-derive — this redirects the fix)
+The menu BUILDER is already correct. `cargo test -p kanban-app --bins menu` passes all 14 tests, including `navigation_submenu_contains_all_nine_nav_commands` and the accelerator tests — proving `collect_menu_entries` over the real composed registry yields a `Navigation` group with all nine `nav.*` (incl. `nav.jump`), and `resolve_accelerator` gives valid accelerators in cua and vim. The nav.yaml metadata is present and correct (`crates/swissarmyhammer-focus/builtin/commands/nav.yaml`, each nav.* has `menu: { path: [Navigation], ... }`).
+
+So the metadata/builder hypothesis in the original card is WRONG. The real bug is BUILD-ORDER / TIMING:
+- `apps/kanban-app/src/main.rs::setup_app` calls `build_initial_menu` (~line 144) EARLY, building the OS menu from the registry as it exists at that moment.
+- The focus/nav commands get composed into the registry LATER — see `apps/kanban-app/src/state.rs` registry composition (~665-669) and `reload_command_overrides` (~949), which runs during `auto_open_board`, AFTER `build_initial_menu`.
+- `reload_command_overrides` swaps the registry but does NOT call `rebuild_menu`. Nothing rebuilds the menu after the nav/focus commands land in the registry.
+- Result: the OS menu is built without the nav commands and never refreshed; meanwhile the `s`/nav KEYBINDINGS work because they resolve from the (later-populated) registry/scope at dispatch time. This is the exact asymmetry the user observed: "`s` jump works but isn't shown in navigation."
+
+## Work
+- Make the menu reflect the fully-composed registry: rebuild the menu after the nav/focus commands are registered (e.g. call `rebuild_menu`/`rebuild_menu_async` after `reload_command_overrides` / after board substrate composition), OR defer/repeat `build_initial_menu` until the registry is complete. Pick the approach that guarantees the Navigation submenu is populated in the running app.
+- `menu.rs`: `build_menu_from_commands`, `collect_menu_entries`, `rebuild_menu`/`rebuild_menu_async`, `update_menu_enabled_state`.
+
+## TDD (RED first)
+Write a failing test at the integration seam that exercises the REAL startup/board-open sequence (not just `collect_menu_entries` in isolation, which already passes). The test must prove that AFTER the registry is composed the way the running app composes it (initial menu build BEFORE nav/focus commands, then registry composition / `reload_command_overrides`), the menu that the app would display contains the Navigation submenu with the nine nav.* commands. The existing unit test passes because it builds the menu from an already-complete registry — the new test must reproduce the early-build-then-late-registry ordering and show the menu is stale, then go green once a rebuild is wired in.
+
+## Acceptance
+- Nav commands appear under a Navigation menu with their accelerators in the actually-running app (build order fixed).
+- New regression test reproduces the build-order bug (RED before the fix) and is GREEN after.
+- `cargo test -p kanban-app --bins menu` stays green.
\ No newline at end of file

The test must prove that AFTER the registry is composed the way the running app composes it (initial menu build BEFORE nav/focus commands, then registry composition / `reload_command_overrides`), the menu that the app would display contains the Navigation submenu with the nine nav.* commands. -

forward_patch:
--- original
+++ modified
@@ -5,25 +5,18 @@
 position_ordinal: '8280'
 title: Navigation menu does not list the motion commands
 ---
-The native Navigation menu does not show the motion/nav commands (nav.up/down/left/right/first/last/jump/drillIn/drillOut etc.) even though the keybindings work.
-
-## Investigation finding (do NOT re-derive — this redirects the fix)
-The menu BUILDER is already correct. `cargo test -p kanban-app --bins menu` passes all 14 tests, including `navigation_submenu_contains_all_nine_nav_commands` and the accelerator tests — proving `collect_menu_entries` over the real composed registry yields a `Navigation` group with all nine `nav.*` (incl. `nav.jump`), and `resolve_accelerator` gives valid accelerators in cua and vim. The nav.yaml metadata is present and correct (`crates/swissarmyhammer-focus/builtin/commands/nav.yaml`, each nav.* has `menu: { path: [Navigation], ... }`).
+REOPENED 2026-06-06 — prior fix was WRONG and discarded. The native Navigation menu does not list the motion/nav commands (nav.up/down/left/right/first/last/jump/drillIn/drillOut), and the command palette is also missing from the OS menu.

-So the metadata/builder hypothesis in the original card is WRONG. The real bug is BUILD-ORDER / TIMING:
-- `apps/kanban-app/src/main.rs::setup_app` calls `build_initial_menu` (~line 144) EARLY, building the OS menu from the registry as it exists at that moment.
-- The focus/nav commands get composed into the registry LATER — see `apps/kanban-app/src/state.rs` registry composition (~665-669) and `reload_command_overrides` (~949), which runs during `auto_open_board`, AFTER `build_initial_menu`.
-- `reload_command_overrides` swaps the registry but does NOT call `rebuild_menu`. Nothing rebuilds the menu after the nav/focus commands land in the registry.
-- Result: the OS menu is built without the nav commands and never refreshed; meanwhile the `s`/nav KEYBINDINGS work because they resolve from the (later-populated) registry/scope at dispatch time. This is the exact asymmetry the user observed: "`s` jump works but isn't shown in navigation."
+## OWNER CORRECTION (authoritative — supersedes all prior notes)
+The CommandService MUST specify OS-menu placement. The plugins/command-service exist to REPLACE the builtin YAML (`compose_registry!` / `nav.yaml`). The OS menu must be built FROM the CommandService catalogue, and each command's `menu` (MenuPlacement) must be carried THROUGH the service catalogue.

-## Work
-- Make the menu reflect the fully-composed registry: rebuild the menu after the nav/focus commands are registered (e.g. call `rebuild_menu`/`rebuild_menu_async` after `reload_command_overrides` / after board substrate composition), OR defer/repeat `build_initial_menu` until the registry is complete. Pick the approach that guarantees the Navigation submenu is populated in the running app.
-- `menu.rs`: `build_menu_from_commands`, `collect_menu_entries`, `rebuild_menu`/`rebuild_menu_async`, `update_menu_enabled_state`.
+The discarded approach (do NOT repeat): a prior attempt made `state.rs::sync_commands_registry_from_metadata` OVERLAY the builtin `compose_registry!` YAML on top of the CommandService snapshot (new `compose_facade_registry` / `overlay_defs`) to re-inject the nav menu metadata. That is inconsistent with the architecture — it props up the YAML the service is meant to replace. All of that code was reverted to HEAD.

-## TDD (RED first)
-Write a failing test at the integration seam that exercises the REAL startup/board-open sequence (not just `collect_menu_entries` in isolation, which already passes). The test must prove that AFTER the registry is composed the way the running app composes it (initial menu build BEFORE nav/focus commands, then registry composition / `reload_command_overrides`), the menu that the app would display contains the Navigation submenu with the nine nav.* commands. The existing unit test passes because it builds the menu from an already-complete registry — the new test must reproduce the early-build-then-late-registry ordering and show the menu is stale, then go green once a rebuild is wired in.
+## Correct goal
+Make commands available on the OS menu via the CommandService — including:
+- Navigation (the nine nav.* motion commands), AND
+- the command palette opener (currently absent from the OS menu entirely).
+The service catalogue is the single source of truth; the menu reads from it. If nav/focus command metadata (incl. `menu` placement) does not currently reach the service catalogue, the fix is to make it reach the service — not to overlay YAML.

-## Acceptance
-- Nav commands appear under a Navigation menu with their accelerators in the actually-running app (build order fixed).
-- New regression test reproduces the build-order bug (RED before the fix) and is GREEN after.
-- `cargo test -p kanban-app --bins menu` stays green.
\ No newline at end of file
+## Next step
+Re-investigate (architecturally-correct lens): how command `menu` placement is supposed to flow into the CommandService catalogue and out to `apps/kanban-app/src/menu.rs`; why nav.* (and the palette command) menu placement does not reach the service catalogue today; and the minimal change so the service carries it. TDD, RED first. This card is now part of the broader "commands on the OS menu" effort the owner is driving next.
\ No newline at end of file

+The native Navigation menu does not show the motion/nav commands (nav.up/down/left/right/first/last/jump/drillIn/drillOut etc.) even though the keybindings work.

-## OWNER CORRECTION (authoritative — supersedes all prior notes)
-The CommandService MUST specify OS-menu placement. The plugins/command-service exist to REPLACE the builtin YAML (`compose_registry!` / `nav.yaml`). The OS menu must be built FROM the CommandService catalogue, and each command's `menu` (MenuPlacement) must be carried THROUGH the service catalogue.
+## Investigation finding (do NOT re-derive — this redirects the fix)
+The menu BUILDER is already correct. `cargo test -p kanban-app --bins menu` passes all 14 tests, including `navigation_submenu_contains_all_nine_nav_commands` and the accelerator tests — proving `collect_menu_entries` over the real composed registry yields a `Navigation` group with all nine `nav.*` (incl. `nav.jump`), and `resolve_accelerator` gives valid accelerators in cua and vim. The nav.yaml metadata is present and correct (`crates/swissarmyhammer-focus/builtin/commands/nav.yaml`, each nav.* has `menu: { path: [Navigation], ... }`).

-The discarded approach (do NOT repeat): a prior attempt made `state.rs::sync_commands_registry_from_metadata` OVERLAY the builtin `compose_registry!` YAML on top of the CommandService snapshot (new `compose_facade_registry` / `overlay_defs`) to re-inject the nav menu metadata. That is inconsistent with the architecture — it props up the YAML the service is meant to replace. All of that code was reverted to HEAD.
+So the metadata/builder hypothesis in the original card is WRONG. The real bug is BUILD-ORDER / TIMING:
+- `apps/kanban-app/src/main.rs::setup_app` calls `build_initial_menu` (~line 144) EARLY, building the OS menu from the registry as it exists at that moment.
+- The focus/nav commands get composed into the registry LATER — see `apps/kanban-app/src/state.rs` registry composition (~665-669) and `reload_command_overrides` (~949), which runs during `auto_open_board`, AFTER `build_initial_menu`.
+- `reload_command_overrides` swaps the registry but does NOT call `rebuild_menu`. Nothing rebuilds the menu after the nav/focus commands land in the registry.
+- Result: the OS menu is built without the nav commands and never refreshed; meanwhile the `s`/nav KEYBINDINGS work because they resolve from the (later-populated) registry/scope at dispatch time. This is the exact asymmetry the user observed: "`s` jump works but isn't shown in navigation."

-## Correct goal
-Make commands available on the OS menu via the CommandService — including:
-- Navigation (the nine nav.* motion commands), AND
-- the command palette opener (currently absent from the OS menu entirely).
-The service catalogue is the single source of truth; the menu reads from it. If nav/focus command metadata (incl. `menu` placement) does not currently reach the service catalogue, the fix is to make it reach the service — not to overlay YAML.
+## Work
+- Make the menu reflect the fully-composed registry: rebuild the menu after the nav/focus commands are registered (e.g. call `rebuild_menu`/`rebuild_menu_async` after `reload_command_overrides` / after board substrate composition), OR defer/repeat `build_initial_menu` until the registry is complete. Pick the approach that guarantees the Navigation submenu is populated in the running app.
+- `menu.rs`: `build_menu_from_commands`, `collect_menu_entries`, `rebuild_menu`/`rebuild_menu_async`, `update_menu_enabled_state`.
+
+## TDD (RED first)
+Write a failing test at the integration seam that exercises the REAL startup/board-open sequence (not just `collect_menu_entries` in isolation, which already passes). The test must prove that AFTER the registry is composed the way the running app composes it (initial menu build BEFORE nav/focus commands, then registry composition / `reload_command_overrides`), the menu that the app would display contains the Navigation submenu with the nine nav.* commands. The existing unit test passes because it builds the menu from an already-complete registry — the new test must reproduce the early-build-then-late-registry ordering and show the menu is stale, then go green once a rebuild is wired in.

-## Next step
-Re-investigate (architecturally-correct lens): how command `menu` placement is supposed to flow into the CommandService catalogue and out to `apps/kanban-app/src/menu.rs`; why nav.* (and the palette command) menu placement does not reach the service catalogue today; and the minimal change so the service carries it. TDD, RED first. This card is now part of the broader "commands on the OS menu" effort the owner is driving next.
\ No newline at end of file
+## Acceptance
+- Nav commands appear under a Navigation menu with their accelerators in the actually-running app (build order fixed).
+- New regression test reproduces the build-order bug (RED before the fix) and is GREEN after.
+- `cargo test -p kanban-app --bins menu` stays green.

--- original

@@ -19,4 +19,11 @@

 ## Next step

+Re-investigate (architecturally-correct lens): how command `menu` placement is supposed to flow into the CommandService catalogue and out to `apps/kanban-app/src/menu.rs`; why nav.* (and the palette command) menu placement does not reach the service catalogue today; and the minimal change so the service carries it. TDD, RED first. This card is now part of the broader "commands on the OS menu" effort the owner is driving next.
+
+## Learnings folded in from duplicate (was #bug 01KTCRQ1ZNMB5WAZSXT4CQZW6Z, now deleted)
+Concrete menu-builder anchors (consistent with "menu reads from the catalogue"):
+- `apps/kanban-app/src/menu.rs`: `build_menu_from_commands` → `build_grouped_submenu(app, "Navigation", menus.get("Navigation"), …)`; `collect_menu_entries` only emits an entry for a command when it carries a `menu` placement with a non-empty `path`. So the blank Navigation menu == no nav.* command in the catalogue carries `Navigation` menu placement at runtime.
+- The nine ids the Navigation submenu must list: `nav.up`, `nav.down`, `nav.left`, `nav.right`, `nav.first`, `nav.last`, `nav.drillIn`, `nav.drillOut`, `nav.jump`.
+- There is a `menu.rs` unit test (`navigation_submenu_contains_all_nine_nav_commands`) that PASSES against an in-test composed registry yet the runtime menu is blank — pinning the gap exactly where the owner correction points: the runtime/service catalogue handed to `build_menu_from_commands` lacks the nav.* menu metadata. (Do NOT "fix" this by overlaying YAML — see owner correction; make the metadata reach the service catalogue.)
+- Palette-on-the-menu overlap is tracked in depth on `01KTCRQ6KJ67FJWYEZFQ6J7R13` (single palette command + add a `menu:` placement). Coordinate the menu-placement plumbing once.
\ No newline at end of file

forward_patch:
--- original
+++ modified
@@ -1,29 +1,47 @@
 ---
 assignees:
 - claude-code
+depends_on:
+- 01KTED5F8DQ2XH5BB0WK1MRR3P
 position_column: todo
 position_ordinal: ce80
-title: Navigation menu does not list the motion commands
+project: ui-command-cleanup
+title: Card A — Register nav.* + palette opener as plugin commands with OS-menu placement
 ---
-REOPENED 2026-06-06 — prior fix was WRONG and discarded. The native Navigation menu does not list the motion/nav commands (nav.up/down/left/right/first/last/jump/drillIn/drillOut), and the command palette is also missing from the OS menu.
+## What
+This is the owner's "start" card. It SUBSUMES the prior "Navigation menu does not list the motion commands" investigation (this card was rewritten in place) and covers the OS-menu-placement half of the palette bug (01KTCRQ6KJ67FJWYEZFQ6J7R13).

-## OWNER CORRECTION (authoritative — supersedes all prior notes)
-The CommandService MUST specify OS-menu placement. The plugins/command-service exist to REPLACE the builtin YAML (`compose_registry!` / `nav.yaml`). The OS menu must be built FROM the CommandService catalogue, and each command's `menu` (MenuPlacement) must be carried THROUGH the service catalogue.
+Register the nine `nav.*` commands and reconcile the palette opener as PLUGIN commands carrying `menu` placement + `keys` + availability, so the OS menu is built FROM the CommandService catalogue. The UI stays presentation (renders the menu, opens the jump overlay, dispatches ids).

-The discarded approach (do NOT repeat): a prior attempt made `state.rs::sync_commands_registry_from_metadata` OVERLAY the builtin `compose_registry!` YAML on top of the CommandService snapshot (new `compose_facade_registry` / `overlay_defs`) to re-inject the nav menu metadata. That is inconsistent with the architecture — it props up the YAML the service is meant to replace. All of that code was reverted to HEAD.
+### Today's (rejected) state to retire
+- `crates/swissarmyhammer-focus/builtin/commands/nav.yaml` defines all nine `nav.*` ids WITH menu placement, but its own header says "Execution lives in React closures in app-shell.tsx (buildNavCommands, buildDrillCommands)" and "the frontend's command merging logic combines YAML-defined and React-defined commands by id." That YAML-merge/overlay is the rejected approach. Migrate these ids into a real plugin and stop merging YAML into the service snapshot.
+- Frontend trash in `apps/kanban-app/ui/src/components/app-shell.tsx`: `NAV_COMMAND_SPEC` (line ~263) + `buildNavCommands` (~324), `buildDrillCommands` (~380), and the inline `nav.jump` def (~798) whose execute is `setJumpOpen(true)`.
+
+### Register as plugin commands (new plugin, e.g. `builtin/plugins/nav-commands/index.ts`, mirroring `builtin/plugins/file-commands/index.ts`)
+- `nav.up/down/left/right/first/last` → backend op `spatial_navigate(window, fq, direction)` in `crates/swissarmyhammer-focus` (operations.rs/server.rs). Carry `keys` + `menu:{path:["Navigation"],group,order}` exactly as nav.yaml has them.
+- `nav.drillIn` / `nav.drillOut` → backend `spatial_drill_in` / `spatial_drill_out`.
+- `nav.focus` → backend `spatial_focus` (the def belongs here; the dedup of its two frontend definitions is Card G).
+- `nav.jump` → handler bus (Card B): plugin owns id/name/keys(`vim:s`,`cua/emacs:Mod+G`)/menu; the webview registers a handler that opens `<JumpToOverlay>` (`setJumpOpen(true)`). Keep `<JumpToOverlay>` and its dismiss path as presentation.
+
+### Palette reconciliation (OS-menu half)
+- Add `menu: { path: ["App"], group, order }` to `ui.palette.open` in `builtin/plugins/ui-commands/index.ts` (it currently has `keys:{cua:"Mod+K",vim:":"}` but NO `menu` — confirmed; that is why the palette isn't on the OS menu).
+- Reconcile the `app.command` / `app.palette` frontend id split: point `apps/kanban-app/ui/src/lib/keybindings.ts` at the registered palette id and delete the STATIC palette entries that duplicate it. (The execution/hotkey-firing failure stays scoped to 01KTCRQ6KJ67FJWYEZFQ6J7R13; THIS card only guarantees the menu affordance + that the catalogue carries the palette command with menu placement.)
+
+### Keep (presentation — do NOT move)
+`apps/kanban-app/src/menu.rs` (OS-menu builder, fed by the service), the jump overlay, the keybinding dispatch handler. Do NOT overlay nav.yaml onto the service snapshot.

-## Correct goal
-Make commands available on the OS menu via the CommandService — including:
-- Navigation (the nine nav.* motion commands), AND
-- the command palette opener (currently absent from the OS menu entirely).
-The service catalogue is the single source of truth; the menu reads from it. If nav/focus command metadata (incl. `menu` placement) does not currently reach the service catalogue, the fix is to make it reach the service — not to overlay YAML.
+## Acceptance Criteria
+- [ ] The nine `nav.*` commands are defined by a PLUGIN (not nav.yaml-merged, not React closures); each carries keys + Navigation `menu` placement; directional/first/last route to `spatial_navigate`, drill to `spatial_drill_in/out`, focus to `spatial_focus`, jump to the handler bus.
+- [ ] `ui.palette.open` carries a `menu` placement (App menu) in ui-commands.
+- [ ] `NAV_COMMAND_SPEC`, `buildNavCommands`, `buildDrillCommands`, and the inline `nav.jump` def are deleted from app-shell.tsx; the jump overlay still opens via the bus.
+- [ ] `crates/swissarmyhammer-focus/builtin/commands/nav.yaml` is retired (or reduced to non-overlay use) so the nav metadata reaches the OS menu THROUGH the service catalogue, not a YAML merge.
+- [ ] The native Navigation submenu renders all nine nav items AND the palette has an App-menu affordance, both built from the service catalogue at runtime.

-## Next step
-Re-investigate (architecturally-correct lens): how command `menu` placement is supposed to flow into the CommandService catalogue and out to `apps/kanban-app/src/menu.rs`; why nav.* (and the palette command) menu placement does not reach the service catalogue today; and the minimal change so the service carries it. TDD, RED first. This card is now part of the broader "commands on the OS menu" effort the owner is driving next.
+## Tests
+- [ ] Rust menu test in `apps/kanban-app/src/menu.rs`: build the menu from the SAME catalogue the app composes at runtime (the production composition path, NOT a hand-built `compose_registry!`); assert "Navigation" has 9 entries and the palette command collects into the App submenu. Regression test that fails before and passes after.
+- [ ] Plugin e2e (mirror `crates/swissarmyhammer-command-service/tests/integration/builtin_ui_commands_e2e.rs`): the nav plugin registers nine nav.* ids with the expected menu placements and backend-op routing; `ui.palette.open` exposes a menu placement.
+- [ ] UI test: dispatching `nav.jump` opens `<JumpToOverlay>` via the handler bus (extend `apps/kanban-app/ui/src/components/jump-to-overlay.browser.test.tsx`); dispatching `nav.up`/`nav.drillIn` invokes the spatial backend op (extend an existing `*.spatial.test.tsx`).
+- [ ] `cargo test -p kanban-app menu` and the relevant vitest files are green.

-## Learnings folded in from duplicate (was #bug 01KTCRQ1ZNMB5WAZSXT4CQZW6Z, now deleted)
-Concrete menu-builder anchors (consistent with "menu reads from the catalogue"):
-- `apps/kanban-app/src/menu.rs`: `build_menu_from_commands` → `build_grouped_submenu(app, "Navigation", menus.get("Navigation"), …)`; `collect_menu_entries` only emits an entry for a command when it carries a `menu` placement with a non-empty `path`. So the blank Navigation menu == no nav.* command in the catalogue carries `Navigation` menu placement at runtime.
-- The nine ids the Navigation submenu must list: `nav.up`, `nav.down`, `nav.left`, `nav.right`, `nav.first`, `nav.last`, `nav.drillIn`, `nav.drillOut`, `nav.jump`.
-- There is a `menu.rs` unit test (`navigation_submenu_contains_all_nine_nav_commands`) that PASSES against an in-test composed registry yet the runtime menu is blank — pinning the gap exactly where the owner correction points: the runtime/service catalogue handed to `build_menu_from_commands` lacks the nav.* menu metadata. (Do NOT "fix" this by overlaying YAML — see owner correction; make the metadata reach the service catalogue.)
-- Palette-on-the-menu overlap is tracked in depth on `01KTCRQ6KJ67FJWYEZFQ6J7R13` (single palette command + add a `menu:` placement). Coordinate the menu-placement plumbing once.
\ No newline at end of file
+## Workflow
+- Use `/tdd` — failing tests first, then implement. Automated tests only. #bug
\ No newline at end of file

 - [ ] The native Navigation submenu renders all nine nav items AND the palette has an App-menu affordance, both built from the service catalogue at runtime.

 ## Tests
-- [ ] Rust menu test in `apps/kanban-app/src/menu.rs`: build the menu from the SAME catalogue the app composes at runtime (the production composition path, NOT a hand-built `compose_registry!`); assert "Navigation" has 9 entries and `app.palette.open` collects into the App submenu. Regression test that fails before and passes after.
-- [ ] Plugin e2e (mirror `crates/swissarmyhammer-command-service/tests/integration/builtin_ui_commands_e2e.rs`): the nav plugin registers nine nav.* ids with the expected menu placements and backend-op routing; `app.palette.open` exposes a menu placement and routes to ui_state.
+- [ ] Rust menu test in `apps/kanban-app/src/menu.rs`: build the menu from the SAME catalogue the app composes at runtime (the production composition path, NOT a hand-built `compose_registry!`); assert "Navigation" has 9 entries and the palette command collects into the App submenu. Regression test that fails before and passes after.
+- [ ] Plugin e2e (mirror `crates/swissarmyhammer-command-service/tests/integration/builtin_ui_commands_e2e.rs`): the nav plugin registers nine nav.* ids with the expected menu placements and backend-op routing; `ui.palette.open` exposes a menu placement.
 - [ ] UI test: dispatching `nav.jump` opens `<JumpToOverlay>` via the handler bus (extend `apps/kanban-app/ui/src/components/jump-to-overlay.browser.test.tsx`); dispatching `nav.up`/`nav.drillIn` invokes the spatial backend op (extend an existing `*.spatial.test.tsx`).
 - [ ] `cargo test -p kanban-app menu` and the relevant vitest files are green.
